$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for icon preprocessing. Plan §5.5/§5.8. This exact transform MUST be reproduced byte-for-byte by the extension's src/shared/config.ts and the OffscreenCanvas rasterization path. Changing any value here is a model-breaking change: retrain + re-export, never edit unilaterally on one side.

render.$comment: How the lab rasterizes SVGs. 'chromium' renders through real Chromium Canvas to match the extension's deployment renderer (the #1 train/serve-skew lever, plan §7).
render.$comment_supersample: Render at input_size*supersample then box-downscale, approximating Canvas drawImage anti-aliasing. Applied identically wherever the fixed transform runs.

color.$comment: Web icons inherit currentColor on arbitrary backgrounds. We composite RGBA over a fixed background, reduce to single-channel luminance, optionally normalize polarity to dark-glyph-on-light, then replicate to 3 channels for the ImageNet-pretrained backbone. Color is a TRAIN-TIME augmentation (augment.py); the deploy transform below is fixed and color-collapsing.
color.$comment_polarity: If true, flip luminance so the image border (assumed background) is the light side. Makes the model invariant to dark-mode / inverted icons.

resize.$comment: Final resize from supersampled raster to input_size. PIL BILINEAR + reduce; the TS side must use the equivalent Canvas drawImage with imageSmoothingQuality high.

normalize.$comment: Applied per-channel after scaling pixels to [0,1]. mean/std 0.5 maps to [-1,1]; identical 3-channel stats because all channels carry the same luminance.
